Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx basic,$0801,$ffff "MAZETHRILLER-5000-2E97" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 jsr $5000 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Maze Thriller (J1).prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Added an init routine at $5000 which: 1. Turns off interrupts from the two CIA chips and acks any pending CIA interrupts. 2. Copies 256 bytes of data from $5030 down to $0300 so that the dump can start at $0400. This allows the game to run correctly. 3. Sets the device number of the current file ($BA) to $01 (tape) to circumvent a copy protection mechanism at $0CCE (check fails if running from disk drive 8). 4. Re-enables raster interrupts and timer NMIs. 5. Jumps to the entry point at $2E97 (it couldn't use RTS because of the copy above overwriting the boot code).
The game is actually dumped when a breakpoint at $2EA4 is hit. This then allows the JSR $0830 at $2EA1 to be NOP'd out and start at the original starting address of $2E97, allowing for quicker startup.
